title: Three ways teams buy TMS software
subtitle: Same job—moving freight—three familiar shapes. Here’s the tradeoff buyers feel in the room, without picking on any one vendor.

columns.badge: 30-day field test · Month-to-month after · No long-term contract
columns.name: Run it like software, not a project

bullets.text: 30-day field test on your fleet—prove it with real dispatch, safety, and billing.
True multi-tenant SaaS: a tab or your phone. No VPC, private hosting, or “call IT before I can log in.”
Onboarding in days—not a quarter-long implementation program.
Pricing on the page: per active truck/driver, a clear monthly floor, optional brokerage add-on.

columns.pricingLine: $20 / active truck or driver (greater of the two) · $100/mo minimum · +$200/mo brokerage add-on

columns.name: The private-footprint world

bullets.text: Organized around your network: VPNs, private links, data centers—or installs that don’t feel like modern SaaS.
Professional services and phased rollouts before you’re living in it end-to-end.
Economics that reward scale, change orders, and long buying cycles—not small-team speed.

columns.pricingLine: Implementation + services are usually the real line item; list price rarely tells the story.

columns.name: Serious cloud, bigger appetite

bullets.text: Real cloud—but often tuned for later-stage ops and larger monthly commits.
Broader products can mean more training and services before the first clean dispatch week.
All-in pricing is still uncommon—you’ll usually talk to sales before you know run-rate.

columns.pricingLine: Often mid-to-high four figures per month before add-ons and services (varies widely).
